page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96-lssd is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and 8-bit integer operations. The system is equipped with 744 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.75 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 16 local drives. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong computational and memory performance. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier read speeds of 7676.9 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 356.43 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a strong write speed of 113.04 GB/sec. The storage and processing architecture supports high-throughput database operations, as evidenced by a top-tier Passmark database score and Redis performance reaching 170.8 million SET operations per second.

The server is highly suited for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads, including large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and data compilation tasks. While its CPU performance is strong across multi-core benchmarks and software compilation, its LLM text generation speeds for large models are average, indicating a tradeoff when utilized for heavy generative AI inference without dedicated GPU acceleration. However, the inclusion of Intel AMX instructions provides acceleration for smaller model prompt processing. The high resource density of memory and local NVMe storage offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring low-latency local data access without the overhead of network-attached storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96-lssd is a memory-optimized server configured with 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 physical cores, a 504 MB L3 cache, and support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The server includes 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives, delivering high-speed local data access. In benchmarks, it exhibits top-tier memory read bandwidth of up to 7676.9 GB/sec and strong multi-core performance. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis throughput reaching 170.8 million SET operations per second. This server is optimized for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and high-throughput web serving, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware for heavy AI text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96-lssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96-lssd features 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and 6167 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and local NVMe storage server optimized for demanding database operations and high-throughput data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors
6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 16 integrated drives
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of up to 7676.9 GB/sec
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector math
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data compilation
